title: Autonomous Racing Car
description: Led a team of 30 students in developing a fully autonomous vehicle and implemented Edge AI for race trajectory.
markdown:
## What
An AI-powered trajectory planning system for ARECE, an autonomous racing vehicle competing in Formula Student Driverless competitions. Reduced trajectory computation time from 17.31 seconds to <1ms per segment using machine learning.

## How
Generated a large synthetic dataset of racing circuits and computed optimal baselines using minimum curvature trajectory optimization based on vehicle dynamics. Each track was split into overlapping segments via a sliding-window approach, with a tuned 'foresight' (fore/aft context window) so the network could see upcoming and preceding track geometry rather than just the current point. Track geometry was encoded per boundary-normal as length, angular change, and angle offset, avoiding raw curvature which is unstable near hairpins and undefined on straights. Designed and trained a feed-forward neural network on these track/trajectory pairs, predicting multiple neighboring waypoints per window and averaging overlapping predictions across windows to produce a smooth racing line without expensive post-hoc filtering. Integrated real-time inference (<1ms) into a ROS2 pipeline, receiving cone positions from perception and publishing waypoint commands to vehicle control.

## Result
Production-ready system achieving real-time operation for competitive autonomous racing, cutting trajectory generation time by several orders of magnitude versus iterative optimal-control optimization. Successfully replaced computationally expensive iterative optimization with neural network inference, validated through simulation and team integration. Created a reusable synthetic track generation framework for training data augmentation as technical lead coordinating across Manufacturing, Perception, Trajectory, and DevOps teams.

### AI Usage (Automation)
AI tools (like code assistants) were used for boilerplate code, formatting, scripting, and automation tasks. The core ML work (state-of-the-art trajectory optimization research, synthetic data generation pipeline, neural network architecture design, model training, and real-time inference implementation) was designed and developed by the team from scratch.
